rationale: The signals show strong, sustained interest in crested gecko morphs overall, with heavy search volume around cappuccino, lilly white, and axanthic variants. However, most raw signals are commerce-driven (MorphMarket listings, YouTube unboxings, price queries) or general awareness content (care guides, lists). The already-published slug list shows we've covered cappuccino genetics, super-cappuccino risks, lilly-white breeding, and axanthic strategy extensively. YouTube reveals recurring frustration themes: "why so much hate for cappuccino geckos," "super cappuccino is a scam," and warnings about which pairings to avoid. This suggests room for a corrective piece on breeding decisions and cost/benefit reality. No forum heat or Reddit activity in this run, limiting demand signals. I am rejecting pure listicles and care content, plus anything that duplicates existing slugs. Remaining viable angles are narrow, genetics-grounded breeding decisions where confusion exists.
